questions.prompt: Which is the BEST first step when receiving a device for repair?

choices.text: Start disassembly immediately
Verify device identity and customer details
Replace the battery
Run a display test first

questions.prompt: What should you do if the device shows signs of liquid exposure?

choices.text: Proceed with repair normally
Document indicators and follow liquid-damage SOP
Charge the phone to confirm power
Factory reset immediately

questions.prompt: It is acceptable to skip cosmetic condition documentation if the device powers on.
Before beginning any repair work, what should be confirmed regarding customer data?

choices.text: Customer’s wallpaper preference
Authorization/consent and correct device ownership details
Bluetooth name is set
Ringtone volume is max

questions.prompt: Which is the BEST reason to document device condition (scratches/dents) at intake?

choices.text: It is optional; documentation is only for powered-off devices
To reduce repair time by skipping checks
To avoid disputes and ensure proper accountability
To improve Wi-Fi performance

questions.prompt: If a device is severely damaged (e.g., swollen battery, exposed internals), what is the BEST action?

choices.text: Proceed quickly to finish before it gets worse
Isolate the device, follow safety SOP, and escalate as required
Try to charge it to see if it powers on
Submerge it in alcohol to clean it

questions.prompt: Intake triage should include verifying basic functionality when safe (e.g., power, touch, buttons) before repair.
What is the PRIMARY safety reason for using eye protection during backglass work?

choices.text: To see better under bright lights
To prevent glass shards from entering eyes
To reduce screen glare
To improve adhesive curing

questions.prompt: Before applying heat for backglass removal, you should FIRST:

choices.text: Install the new backglass
Remove SIM tray / note device condition / protect sensitive areas
Run a factory reset
Charge the battery to 100%

questions.prompt: What is the BEST practice when removing broken glass fragments?

choices.text: Use bare hands for better grip
Use approved tools/PPE and control debris to avoid contamination
Blow shards off with your mouth
Shake the device over a trash bin

questions.prompt: Excessive heat can increase the risk of internal component damage during backglass removal.
After backglass removal, what should be done before installing a replacement backglass?

choices.text: Leave old adhesive if it still feels sticky
Clean the surface, remove old adhesive residue, and inspect the frame for damage
Apply adhesive directly over dust
Skip inspection to reduce cycle time

questions.prompt: Which is the MOST critical display check after replacement?

choices.text: Wallpaper color accuracy
Touch responsiveness across the entire grid
Ringtone volume
Bluetooth name

questions.prompt: Dead pixels and touch issues should be documented and handled via rework/escalation SOP.
After installing a new display, which step should be done BEFORE final sealing?

choices.text: Run a full functional test (touch, brightness, sensors if applicable)
Apply permanent adhesive immediately
Ship the device to speed turnaround
Lower brightness to 0%

questions.prompt: What is the BEST practice for checking touch performance?

choices.text: Test only the center area
Test the full grid including edges and corners
Skip touch test if display turns on
Only test with gloves off

questions.prompt: If the display passes tests but shows visual gaps, the device should be reworked before closing.
